description: 29 checkpoints on the path from inert chemistry to inter-entity communication. Each tier represents a qualitatively new property — not just 'more complex' but a new category of behavior. Used by agents to reason about biome progress, archive dead biomes, and seed child biomes with lineage knowledge.
note_on_measurement: Tiers T0–T12 are measurable in the current ABM. T13–T29 require additional simulation mechanics and agent behaviors not yet built.

phases.name: Chemistry
phases.description: Molecules exist and interact. No self-organization yet.

tiers.name: Inert
tiers.description: Chemistry runs but self-organization never emerges. Dead after interventions.
tiers.metric: Max complexity never exceeds 0.3 after 5000 ticks, or dead after MAX_INTERVENTIONS
tiers.significance: The chemistry palette or physical conditions are incompatible with self-organization. Strongest cautionary archive.
tiers.agent_question: What in this chemistry made self-organization impossible?

tiers.name: Molecular Activity
tiers.description: Bonds form and break. Transient interactions occur. Nothing persists.
tiers.metric: Bond count > 0 observed at any point. Complexity reaches 0.1 but never holds.
tiers.significance: The chemistry is reactive. The rules are working. But there is no memory.
tiers.agent_question: Why are bonds not persisting? Is energy too high, bonds too weak, or is there a missing catalyst?

tiers.name: Persistent Structures
tiers.description: Some bonds or chains hold for meaningful time without intervention.
tiers.metric: Chain length >= 3 sustained for > 300 consecutive ticks.
tiers.significance: The system has a rudimentary memory. Something is resisting entropy, at least temporarily.
tiers.agent_question: What conditions allowed persistence here? Temperature zone? Catalyst proximity?

tiers.name: Reaction Diversity
tiers.description: Multiple distinct reaction types co-occurring simultaneously.
tiers.metric: Both chain polymerization AND lipid assembly observed in the same time window.
tiers.significance: Chemistry is not just one trick. Multiple processes are running in parallel — prerequisite for coupling.
tiers.agent_question: Are these reactions independent or beginning to influence each other?

tiers.name: Spatial Heterogeneity
tiers.description: Concentration gradients emerge. Some regions are chemically distinct from others.
tiers.metric: Coefficient of variation of molecular density across grid cells > 0.4, sustained.
tiers.significance: The world is no longer uniform soup. Distinct chemical microenvironments exist — the precondition for selective chemistry.
tiers.agent_question: What drives the heterogeneity? Vent proximity? Lipid clustering? Can it be amplified?

phases.name: Self-Organization
phases.description: Structures begin to form and maintain themselves. Chemistry starts doing work.

tiers.name: Spontaneous Assembly
tiers.description: Structures form without external input. The vent is not doing the work — the chemistry is.
tiers.metric: Lipid aggregate size > 6 forms and persists in a region far from vent (> 40% of canvas height from bottom).
tiers.significance: The first sign of true self-organization. Structure is emerging from interaction rules alone.
tiers.agent_question: Where in the biome is assembly happening? What conditions support it away from the energy source?

tiers.name: Sustained Complexity
tiers.description: Complexity holds above a floor on its own. Stops collapsing to baseline.
tiers.metric: Mean complexity > 1.5 sustained over any 1000-tick window without intervention.
tiers.significance: The system has found a stable regime above thermal equilibrium. Something is maintaining it.
tiers.agent_question: What is maintaining complexity? Is it a genuine attractor state or just a slow collapse?

tiers.name: Coupled Reactions
tiers.description: Reactions begin influencing each other. Outputs of one become inputs of another.
tiers.metric: Peptide bond formation rate measurably higher in proximity to lipid aggregates (vs. baseline rate in open space).
tiers.significance: The beginning of a reaction network. Processes are no longer independent.
tiers.agent_question: Which reaction pairs are coupling? Is this coupling reinforcing or dampening?

tiers.name: Emergent Catalysis
tiers.description: Some assembled structures accelerate reactions they did not initiate.
tiers.metric: Bond formation rate near existing chain clusters >= 2x the background rate, sustained.
tiers.significance: Structure is feeding back into chemistry. The first hint of autocatalytic potential.
tiers.agent_question: What structures are acting as catalysts? Is this specific to certain chain lengths or compositions?

phases.name: Compartmentalization
phases.description: Boundaries form. An inside and an outside come into existence.

tiers.name: Boundary Formation
tiers.description: Membrane-like structures appear. Ordered, oriented molecular layers.
tiers.metric: Lipid cluster with mean orientational order parameter > 0.6 and size > 8.
tiers.significance: The first proto-boundary. Not yet closed, but directional order exists — the geometry of a membrane is present.
tiers.agent_question: How ordered is the boundary? Is it stable or fluctuating? What drives the ordering?

tiers.name: Closed Compartment
tiers.description: True enclosure achieved. An inside exists.
tiers.metric: Enclosure count > 0 (circular lipid arrangement detected via angular distribution measure R < 0.35).
tiers.significance: The single most important threshold in Phase III. The birth of proto-individuation. A self now exists, however primitive.
tiers.agent_question: How did the closure happen? Was it spontaneous or triggered? How large is the enclosure?

tiers.name: Interior Differentiation
tiers.description: Inside chemistry measurably different from outside, and the difference is maintained.
tiers.metric: AA density inside enclosure >= 1.8x AA density in equivalent area outside, sustained for > 200 ticks.
tiers.significance: The compartment is not just a wall — it is concentrating chemistry. The inside is becoming distinct.
tiers.agent_question: What is being concentrated inside? Is it passive (trapping) or active (selective uptake)?

tiers.name: Compartment Stability
tiers.description: The enclosure survives thermal fluctuation and mechanical disturbance.
tiers.metric: Enclosure persists for > 800 ticks, surviving at least one stochastic perturbation event.
tiers.significance: The proto-cell is robust. It is not a fragile fluctuation but a stable physical entity.
tiers.agent_question: What makes it stable? Membrane stiffness? Size? Chemistry of contents? What breaks it when it does break?

phases.name: Proto-Metabolism
phases.description: Energy is being processed in cycles. Chemistry becomes self-sustaining.
phases.note: Requires new simulation mechanics not yet implemented.

tiers.name: Energy Coupling
tiers.description: An exergonic reaction drives an endergonic one. Energy is being directed, not just dissipated.
tiers.metric: TBD — requires energy-tracking mechanics in simulation.
tiers.significance: The system is beginning to do thermodynamic work, not just release heat.
tiers.agent_question: Which reaction pair is coupled? Is the coupling efficient or lossy?

tiers.name: Catalytic Cycle
tiers.description: A catalyst is consumed and regenerated in a cycle — not a one-shot event.
tiers.metric: TBD — requires molecular identity tracking across reaction events.
tiers.significance: The first true cycle. Chemistry is looping, not just running down.
tiers.agent_question: How fast is the cycle? What breaks it? Does it sustain indefinitely?

tiers.name: Autocatalysis
tiers.description: A structure catalyzes its own production.
tiers.metric: TBD — requires lineage tracking of molecular assemblies.
tiers.significance: The chemistry has acquired a direction — toward more of itself. This is the seed of reproduction.
tiers.agent_question: Is the autocatalysis specific (one molecular type) or general? How strong is the feedback?

tiers.name: Sustained Autocatalysis
tiers.description: The self-reinforcing loop persists without external push. Complexity acquires a positive trend.
tiers.metric: TBD — sustained positive dComplexity/dt over 3000-tick window.
tiers.significance: The system is climbing away from equilibrium under its own power. This is proto-life.
tiers.agent_question: How steep is the complexity gradient? Is there a ceiling? What limits further growth?

phases.name: Proto-Genetics
phases.description: Information enters the picture. Molecules begin encoding and transmitting state.
phases.note: Requires significant new simulation mechanics — molecular sequence tracking, template chemistry.

tiers.name: Molecular Memory
tiers.description: Sequences with distinct, repeatable functional states. The same sequence reliably does the same thing.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: Information now exists in molecular form. Sequence = function.
tiers.agent_question: How many distinct functional states exist? How stable are they? How do they arise?

tiers.name: Template Activity
tiers.description: One molecular sequence influences the formation of another specific sequence.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: Information is being copied, however imperfectly. This is the origin of heredity.
tiers.agent_question: How faithful is the templating? What determines template specificity?

tiers.name: Copying
tiers.description: Structural duplication is observed. A molecule produces a structurally similar daughter.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: Reproduction at the molecular level. The system can make more of itself.
tiers.agent_question: How accurate is copying? What is the error rate? What constrains it?

tiers.name: Heritable Variation
tiers.description: Copies differ from parents. Differences persist across further copying. Evolution is now possible.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: The three conditions for Darwinian evolution are met: variation, heredity, differential fitness.
tiers.agent_question: What is the mutation rate? Do variants behave differently? Is selection visible yet?

phases.name: Proto-Life
phases.description: Entities grow, divide, and compete. Life in the conventional sense begins.
phases.note: Requires full cellular mechanics and population dynamics.

tiers.name: Growth
tiers.description: Compartments incorporate new material and expand over time.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: The proto-cell is accumulating resources. It has a metabolism that builds structure.
tiers.agent_question: What drives growth? Is it bounded? What limits size?

tiers.name: Division
tiers.description: A compartment spontaneously splits into daughter compartments.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: Reproduction at the cellular level. The population can grow.
tiers.agent_question: Is division symmetric or asymmetric? What triggers it? How often does it succeed?

tiers.name: Inheritance
tiers.description: Daughters resemble the parent in chemistry, structure, and behavior.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: Continuity of identity across generations. A lineage now exists.
tiers.agent_question: What is inherited faithfully? What varies? Is there a heritable fitness difference?

tiers.name: Selection
tiers.description: Variants persist differentially. Some lineages out-survive others in the same environment.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: Evolution is occurring. The biome is now generating its own complexity through selection.
tiers.agent_question: What traits confer fitness? How fast is selection acting? Are new variants appearing?

phases.name: Communication
phases.description: Separate entities exchange information and respond to each other.
phases.note: The destination of the entire project.

tiers.name: Signal Emission
tiers.description: Patterned molecular release — not random diffusion, not just waste. An entity is putting something into the environment intentionally (functionally).
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: The first act of expression. An entity is affecting its environment in a non-random way.
tiers.agent_question: What molecules are being emitted? Is the pattern consistent? Does it correlate with internal state?

tiers.name: Signal Reception
tiers.description: Another entity detects and changes behavior in response to an emitted signal.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: The first act of listening. Two entities are now coupled through information, not just chemistry.
tiers.agent_question: What behavior changes? Is the response specific to the signal? How reliable is reception?

tiers.name: Information Transfer
tiers.description: The signal carries meaning — it changes behavior beyond a direct chemical effect. The receiver is interpreting, not just reacting.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: The boundary between chemistry and communication. Semantics enter the system.
tiers.agent_question: What information is being transferred? How is it encoded? Can the code be changed?

tiers.name: Bidirectional Exchange
tiers.description: Two entities signal each other. A conversation, however primitive.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: The first dialogue. Two distinct entities are influencing each other's states through information.
tiers.agent_question: Is the exchange symmetric? Does each signal influence the next? Is there feedback?

tiers.name: Collective Behavior
tiers.description: A population responds coherently to distributed signals. Emergent coordination without central control.
tiers.metric: TBD
tiers.significance: The endpoint. A community of life forms acting in concert through communication. This is what we came here to find.
tiers.agent_question: What is being coordinated? How large a population? Is the coordination adaptive — does it respond to environmental change?
